summary: Ran comprehensive pytest suite (29 tests) against CVForge backend covering health, auth, CV CRUD + AI generation, templates, free-tier limit, premium gating, PDF export, Stripe checkout session + status, and webhook signature validation. 28/29 passed. Sole failure is GET /api/payments/checkout/status/{session_id} returning 500 instead of handling Stripe lookup errors gracefully.

minor.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error when Stripe API fails to retrieve the session (emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutError: 'No such checkout.session: cs_test_...'). Observed immediately after a successful POST /api/payments/checkout/session in the same request flow. The endpoint should catch the CheckoutError and return a proper HTTP error (e.g. 502/503 or 404) rather than leaking an unhandled exception. Root cause: line 659 in server.py calls `await stripe.get_checkout_status(session_id)` without try/except; the Stripe sandbox key 'sk_test_emergent' occasionally returns 'No such session' even for sessions it just created.

action_items: Wrap `await stripe.get_checkout_status(session_id)` in GET /api/payments/checkout/status/{session_id} with try/except CheckoutError, returning HTTPException(502, 'Impossible de vérifier le paiement') or similar instead of 500. Ideally also return the last-known DB txn status when Stripe is unreachable.
critical_code_review_comments: payments/checkout/status handler has no error handling around Stripe SDK call – unhandled exceptions become 500s (confirmed in logs).
JWT_SECRET in /app/backend/.env is the placeholder 'change-me-cvforge-prod-secret-2026' – acceptable for dev but must be rotated before prod.
bcrypt password hashing, uuid IDs, JSON-safe responses (no _id leak), UUID-based user/CV ids, and premium/template gating are all implemented correctly.

GET /api/cv/{id} returns the raw DB document (including raw_input). That is fine, but consider using a response_model to avoid accidental field leakage in future (e.g. if password_hash ever lands on that collection).
server.py is 738 lines – approaching the 700-line split threshold. Consider separating auth, cv, payments, and PDF builder into modules.
optimize_cv_with_ai has solid JSON-robustness fallback and fence-stripping.
Free-tier limit increment (`$inc: cvs_generated`) happens after successful CV insert – correct ordering.
Stripe checkout_status does not roll back is_premium if Stripe returns 'expired' or 'unpaid' after a prior 'paid' (minor).
test_credentials: Dynamic: test_<uuid>@cvforge.dev / TestPass123! (registered at runtime). Sample persisted: testuser@cvforge.dev / TestPass123!
seed_data_creation: Two ephemeral users and one CV created per run via registration; CV deleted at end of CV test class. Payment transactions rows remain in db.payment_transactions (session_id recorded with status=initiated).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_cvforge_backend.py -v` after main agent adds try/except around stripe.get_checkout_status in server.py (line 659). All other 28 assertions (auth register/login/me, duplicate handling, CV generation via GPT-5.2, free tier 402, premium template gating, ownership 404, template update, PDF download with %PDF magic-bytes + Content-Type check, Stripe session creation, webhook 400 on bad sig) currently pass.
rca_of_the_issue: Reproduction: 1) POST /api/payments/checkout/session with valid auth & {package_id:'premium_monthly', origin_url:'https://...'} returns 200 with a session_id. 2) Immediately GET /api/payments/checkout/status/{session_id} with same token -> 500. Backend log shows `emergentintegrations.payments.stripe.checkout.CheckoutError: Failed to retrieve session status: No such checkout.session: cs_test_...`. Root cause: the endpoint does not catch CheckoutError, so FastAPI surfaces it as 500. Mitigation: wrap the SDK call in try/except, return an HTTPException with an appropriate code (e.g. 502), and optionally fall back to the DB transaction's last-known payment_status when Stripe is unavailable.
